safety_use_cases.label: Ergonomic risk detection (improper bending and overreaching)
safety_use_cases.description: Detect and score improper bending and overreaching ergonomics to surface risky motions for coaching, using a defined thresholding method.

evidence.quote: Okay, so the way that we categorize or determine threshold level on improper bend and overreaching, so our ergonomic use cases, is going to be with the Posture Risk Index scoring method, certain severity levels assigned to that.

safety_use_cases.label: PIT–pedestrian proximity monitoring
safety_use_cases.description: Monitor and flag close interactions between forklifts and pedestrians, including when a stationary person is near a moving PIT and during scanning at dock plates.

evidence.quote: When we work, like sometimes we have two people working on a similar trailer.

evidence.quote: While the machine is in, uh, is in motion.
evidence.speaker: Blake Sinclair <fake.blake.sinclair@masonlinesupply.com>

evidence.quote: so that thing is a little bit, you know, um, I think it's, it must have showing red flags on that for sure.
evidence.speaker: Blake Sinclair <fake.blake.sinclair@masonlinesupply.com>

evidence.speaker: Blake Sinclair <fake.blake.sinclair@masonlinesupply.com>

evidence.quote: But in the case of a person, a stationary person in a mobile pit will flag.

safety_use_cases.label: PIT–PIT proximity monitoring
safety_use_cases.description: Detect and report close interactions between moving forklifts; identify and reduce PIT-to-PIT proximity incidents.

evidence.quote: So both pits have to be moving to flag.

evidence.quote: So a pit going by a stationary pit will not flag.

evidence.quote: pit-to-pit procs down 40-80%.

nonsafety_use_cases.description: Track extended PIT parking durations that may reflect normal work (e.g., lunch, labeling) and refine camera zones to exclude expected parking areas.

evidence.quote: So the vehicle will be parked for a while before it's actually moved.
evidence.speaker: Harper Sullivan

evidence.quote: So that I think will always be going up instead of going down.

evidence.quote: Yeah, and I think there's also, Harper, there's some fine-tuning we can do on the camera views for where we expect PIT to be parked, because I know that's changed recently, and where we expect freight to sit and where not to sit, because I know today, I painted the brush quite broad to try and capture everything, and then now we're just going to narrow it down a bit to not focus on some of those areas.
